Very thoroughly read ALL scraping-related scripts in D:\ClaudeWorkspace\08_客户跟进\Okki\scripts\ and analyze the complete data pipeline.

Read these files fully:
1. extract-all-v2.mjs - the full extraction script
2. deep-scrape-p0p1.mjs - the P0/P1 deep scraper
3. daily-pipeline.mjs - the daily orchestration pipeline
4. scraper.mjs - the original scraper
5. snapshot.mjs - the index snapshot
6. snapshot-full.mjs - the full snapshot with scrolling
7. diff.mjs - the comparison engine

For each script, identify:
- What data it extracts (index only vs full messages)
- How it handles DOM elements (click, scroll, wait)
- What are the known failure points
- How it deals with virtual scrolling

Then provide a consolidated analysis of:
1. Why the deep scrape only got 6/39 (15%) success rate
2. What specific DOM/virtual-scrolling behavior is blocking it
3. How the window variable data differs from DOM data
4. Which scripts are essential vs redundant

Report under 400 words with specific line numbers for key issues.

This uses the raw `contact.name` from window data.

getConversationList in deep-scrape-p0p1.mjs lines 32-35:
```javascript
const nameMatch = nameText.match(/^(.+?)(?:\d{2,4}[-:\/])/);
const name = nameMatch ? nameMatch[1].trim() : nameText.substring(0, 30).trim() || 'unknown';
```
This regex-extracts the name from the DOM text, which includes the name + timestamp + message preview.

So there could be mismatches where the full name (e.g., "Sarah nell roly Othayi MAMALHY") might be rendered differently in the DOM. But the 5 successful P0 scrapes suggest that the matching does work when the item is rendered.

The critical bug is definitely that `getConversationList` is called once without first scrolling the sidebar to load all items. The fix in `deep-scrape-p0-only.mjs` (`scrollSidebarToLoadAll`) partially addresses this but was never backported to `deep-scrape-p0p1.mjs`.

Here is the consolidated analysis.

---

## Per-Script Breakdown

**extract-all-v2.mjs** (299 lines)
- Data extracted: Index-level from DOM (name, unread, direction via `textContent.includes('[未读]')` at L121), then **full 422-customer structured data from `window.__conversationListFullData__`** at L149 — the only script that accesses this global variable. Merges the two at L199-222, then classifies P0-P3.
- DOM handling: No scrolling. Reads only the ~20-30 `.contact-item-container` elements currently rendered (L111). The window variable is the real data source.
- Failure points: Name extraction from DOM uses regex `^(.+?)(?:\d{2,4}[-:\/])` (L118) which can truncate or fail on non-standard name formats. Message direction detection (L128-132) is heuristic and will miss many cases.

**deep-scrape-p0p1.mjs** (352 lines)
- Data extracted: Full chat messages (after click, scroll-load, identity-verify). Outputs per-message side/content/timestamp/card data.
- DOM handling: Clicks via `scrollIntoView + click` (L41-48), polls header for 6 seconds (L51-58), identity verification checks header text (L66-99), scrolls chat to top repeatedly to defeat virtual scrolling (L122-174).
- Known failure points: **L266-271: `getConversationList` called once with no sidebar scrolling** — only captures ~20-30 rendered DOM items. All 33 skipped customers hit `idx === undefined` at L283. Index refresh at L321-324 only re-queries without scrolling.

**scraper.mjs** (437 lines)
- Data extracted: Index + full messages for first 25 conversations only (L358). Also extracts images and file links.
- DOM handling: Uses fixed 2-second `setTimeout` after click (L302) — no polling, no identity verification. Has scroll-to-bottom for sidebar (L53-63) but limited to 25 items.
- Redundant: Superseded by extract-all-v2 + deep-scrape-p0p1.

**snapshot.mjs** (141 lines) / **snapshot-full.mjs** (167 lines)
- Data extracted: Index-only (name, unread, last message snippet, direction guess).
- snapshot-full adds incremental sidebar scrolling (~80 steps, L99-130) with dedup by name set to handle virtual scrolling.
- Both redundant for the current pipeline since extract-all-v2 gets all 422 customers from window data.

**diff.mjs** (242 lines)
- Data extracted: Change detection between two index snapshots. Independent classification engine (P0-P3 at L28-93). Detects new/changed/removed customers.
- Essential for change-over-time tracking, but operates on snapshot data (DOM-only), so unread status accuracy is limited.

**daily-pipeline.mjs** (61 lines)
- Orchestration only: runs extract-all-v2 then deep-scrape-p0p1 sequentially via `execSync`, 10-minute timeout per phase.

---

## Consolidated Analysis

### 1. Why 6/39 (15%) success rate

The root cause is at **deep-scrape-p0p1.mjs:266-271**. `getConversationList` is called **once** before any sidebar scrolling, returning only the ~20-30 conversation items currently rendered in the virtual-scroll viewport. The `nameToIndex` map is built from this truncated set. When iterating targets at L282-285, any customer whose DOM element is outside the viewport hits `idx === undefined` and is silently skipped. The 5 P0 successes correspond to customers with unread messages that sort to the top of the list and were therefore visible. The single P1 success (Mona almuharraqy) was also in the initial viewport. All 33 skipped names are absent from the initial DOM render.

A secondary factor: the `convList` names come from DOM regex truncation (L33-35), while the P0P1 list names come from `window.__conversationListFullData__` (extract-all-v2 L167), creating potential cross-source mismatches even when items are rendered.

### 2. Specific DOM/virtual-scrolling behavior blocking it

The Xiaoman TM sidebar uses a virtual list: only ~20-30 `.contact-item-container` divs exist in the DOM at any moment. Items outside the visible range are not rendered at all — they have no DOM node, no click target, no text content. `deep-scrape-p0p1.mjs` never scrolls the sidebar to load more items. The fix exists in `deep-scrape-p0-only.mjs:28-108` (`scrollSidebarToLoadAll`) which scrolls in 0.7x-viewport increments with a 3x-stable termination check, but this was never integrated into `deep-scrape-p0p1.mjs`.

### 3. Window variable vs DOM data differences

`window.__conversationListFullData__` (accessed at extract-all-v2 L149) provides all 422 customers with structured fields: `contact.name`, `contact.complianceCountryCode`, `unreadCount` (integer), `lastContactTime` (epoch ms), `latestMessage.content`, `contactRead` (boolean), `mute`, `stayTop`, `block`. The DOM only yields a regex-parsed name, a boolean unread check from the ' [未读]' label substring, and a heuristic message-direction guess. The window variable is authoritative; the DOM adds only the real-time rendering state of the top ~20 items.

### 4. Essential vs redundant scripts

**Essential:** `extract-all-v2.mjs` (sole accessor of the 422-record window dataset), `daily-pipeline.mjs` (orchestration), `diff.mjs` (change detection between runs).

**Redundant:** `scraper.mjs` (limited to 25 items, fixed 2s waits, superseded), `snapshot.mjs` (basic DOM scrape, superseded by snapshot-full), `snapshot-full.mjs` (incremental DOM coverage but extract-all-v2 gets everything from window data anyway).

**Partially broken, needs fix:** `deep-scrape-p0p1.mjs` — missing sidebar pre-scroll at L266-271. The missing scroll logic already exists in `deep-scrape-p0-only.mjs` L28-108 and should be backported. The 33/39 skip rate would drop to near-zero if sidebar scrolling and fuzzy name matching (as in `deep-scrape-p0-only.mjs` L292-302) were added before building `nameToIndex`.
